summary: Strict-mode review FAILS on both dimensions. The verification architecture invents save-source literals ('idle', 'blur') that contradict the domain enum (domain-events.md:115) defined in the same spec's RD-001; this would let Phase 2 emit type-illegal payloads. Required tooling (@testing-library/svelte, Stryker, axe-core) is not installed in promptnotes/package.json yet ~30 PROP-EDIT-* obligations and the Phase 5 mutation gate depend on them. State ownership is incoherent — REQ-EDIT-014 says EditingSessionState is owned by the domain, but editorReducer.ts is a pure UI module that also returns 'EditingSessionState-compatible' state, leaving Phase 2 to guess who owns transitions. REQ-EDIT-025 (blur-save before new-note when dirty) directly contradicts EC-EDIT-008 (Ctrl+N during save-failed dispatches RequestNewNote without blur-save). Multiple REQ-EDITs depend on Timestamp/Body/NoteId Brand types but the spec never says how the UI obtains them given the Rust-side Smart Constructor rule. Phantom 'auto-focus on mount' integration test references a REQ acceptance criterion that does not exist. Several Tier 0 lint rules (no-direct-session-state-write, no-svelte4-writable-for-editor) are referenced as Phase 5 gates but no ESLint config exists. The purity-audit grep is incomplete: it omits crypto, Math.random, performance.now, requestAnimationFrame, fetch, localStorage — all forbidden by §2 yet undetectable by the audit. Banner copy/Discard/Cancel scenario coverage is acceptable, but state-transition contradictions and tooling gaps mean Phase 2 cannot proceed without guessing.
